---
title: "Jank Quickstart"
filters:
  - jank
---

## Basics

```{.jank}
(+ 1 2 3)
```

```{.jank}
(str "Hello from " "Jank!")
```

```{.jank}
(defn square [x] (* x x))
```

```{.jank}
(map square [1 2 3 4 5])
```

## Setup helpers

Define utility functions for rendering.
These blocks evaluate but produce no visible output.

```{.jank output=hidden}
(defn hiccup->html [form]
  (cond
    (string? form) form
    (number? form) (str form)
    (vector? form)
    (let [tag (name (first form))
          has-attrs (map? (second form))
          attrs (if has-attrs (second form) {})
          children (if has-attrs (drop 2 form) (rest form))
          attr-str (apply str
                     (map (fn [[k v]]
                            (str " " (name k) "=\"" v "\""))
                          attrs))]
      (str "<" tag attr-str ">"
           (apply str (map hiccup->html children))
           "</" tag ">"))
    :else (str form)))
```

```{.jank output=hidden}
(defn to-json [v]
  (cond
    (nil? v) "null"
    (true? v) "true"
    (false? v) "false"
    (string? v) (str "\"" v "\"")
    (keyword? v) (str "\"" (name v) "\"")
    (number? v) (str v)
    (vector? v) (str "[" (clojure.string/join ", " (map to-json v)) "]")
    (seq? v) (str "[" (clojure.string/join ", " (map to-json v)) "]")
    (map? v) (str "{"
               (clojure.string/join ", "
                 (map (fn [[k val]]
                        (str (to-json k) ": " (to-json val)))
                      v))
               "}")
    :else (str v)))
```

## Inline SVG

Generate SVG from hiccup-like data in Jank:

```{.jank output=html}
(hiccup->html
  [:svg {:width "300" :height "200" :xmlns "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"}
    [:rect {:width "300" :height "200" :fill "#f0f0f0" :rx "10"}]
    [:circle {:cx "80" :cy "100" :r "50" :fill "steelblue"}]
    [:circle {:cx "160" :cy "100" :r "40" :fill "coral"}]
    [:circle {:cx "230" :cy "100" :r "30" :fill "mediumseagreen"}]
    [:text {:x "150" :y "180" :text-anchor "middle" :font-size "14" :fill "#333"}
      "Generated by Jank"]])
```

## Plotly chart

Build a Plotly.js spec as Jank data structures:

```{.jank output=html}
(let [xs (vec (map #(* 0.5 %) (range 20)))
      ys (vec (map #(+ (* % %) (* -0.5 %)) xs))]
  (str "<div id=\"plot1\"></div>"
       "<script src=\"https://cdn.plot.ly/plotly-2.35.0.min.js\"></script>"
       "<script>Plotly.newPlot('plot1', "
       (to-json {:data [{:x xs :y ys
                         :type "scatter" :mode "lines+markers"
                         :name "y = x² - 0.5x"}]
                 :layout {:title "Quadratic from Jank"
                          :xaxis {:title "x"}
                          :yaxis {:title "y"}}})
       ");</script>"))
```

## Markdown table

Generate a markdown table from computed data:

```{.jank output=markdown}
(let [headers ["x" "x²" "x³"]
      rows (map (fn [x] [(str x) (str (* x x)) (str (* x x x))]) (range 1 8))
      header-line (str "| " (clojure.string/join " | " headers) " |")
      sep-line (str "| " (clojure.string/join " | " (map (fn [_] "---") headers)) " |")
      row-lines (map (fn [row] (str "| " (clojure.string/join " | " row) " |")) rows)]
  (clojure.string/join "\n" (concat [header-line sep-line] row-lines)))
```

## Stdout

Printed output still appears as a code block:

```{.jank}
(do (println "computing...") (* 6 7))
```

## Kindly convention

The [Kindly](https://scicloj.github.io/kindly-noted/) convention uses metadata
to annotate values with their rendering intent. This is compatible with the
Clojure ecosystem (Clay, Portal, etc.).

### Hiccup via Kindly

Annotate a hiccup vector with `^:kind/hiccup` — the filter converts it to HTML automatically
(no `output=html` needed):

```{.jank}
^:kind/hiccup
[:svg {:width "200" :height "120" :xmlns "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"}
  [:rect {:width "200" :height "120" :fill "#e8f4f8" :rx "8"}]
  [:circle {:cx "60" :cy "60" :r "35" :fill "#4a90d9"}]
  [:circle {:cx "140" :cy "60" :r "35" :fill "#d94a4a"}]
  [:text {:x "100" :y "110" :text-anchor "middle" :font-size "12" :fill "#555"}
    "Kindly hiccup"]]
```

The long form `^{:kindly/kind :kind/hiccup}` works too:

```{.jank}
^{:kindly/kind :kind/hiccup}
[:div {:style "padding:12px; background:#f0f0f0; border-radius:8px;"}
  [:b "Hello"] " from " [:em "Kindly"] "!"]
```

### Hidden via Kindly

Use `^:kind/hidden` to suppress output.
Note: Kindly metadata only works on values that can hold metadata (vectors, maps),
not on expression results like strings or vars. For arbitrary code, use `output=hidden`.

```{.jank}
^:kind/hidden
[(def kindly-secret 42)]
```
